entities.description: The pod borer pest causing damage to chickpea

entities.description: The ability of the plant to inhibit the growth or survival of the pest

entities.name: LABORATORY CONDITIONS
entities.description: The controlled environment where the study was conducted

entities.name: DETACHED LEAF ASSAY
entities.description: A technique used to evaluate pest weight gain on separated leaves

entities.name: POD ASSAY
entities.description: A technique used to evaluate the level of pod damage by the pest

entities.name: WEIGHT GAIN
entities.description: The increase in mass of the pest during the leaf assay

entities.name: POD DAMAGE
entities.description: The percentage of pods damaged by the pest

entities.name: PUPAL WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of the pupae after feeding on specific cultivars

entities.name: ICCV 097105
entities.description: A chickpea cultivar showing lower weight gain and pupal weight

entities.name: ICCV 07306
entities.description: A chickpea cultivar showing maximum weight gain and high pod damage

entities.name: ICCV 92944
entities.description: A chickpea cultivar showing the lowest pod damage

entities.name: ICC 3137
entities.description: A chickpea cultivar showing high pod damage

entities.name: LL 550
entities.description: A chickpea cultivar showing high pod damage and highest pupal weight

entities.name: WEEDS
entities.description: Plants suggested for future study to develop management compounds

entities.name: PHEROMONE COMPOUND
entities.description: A chemical compound for the ecofriendly management of the pest

entities.name: REPELLANT COMPOUND
entities.description: A chemical compound to repel the pest for ecofriendly management

entities.name: ECOFRIENDLY MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The sustainable control of Helicoverpa armigera
